title: pelargonium × mariae
intro: Pelargonium × mariae, also known as the Mountain Rose, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, resulting from the cross between Pelargonium tomentosum and Pelargonium peltatum. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of essential oils, which have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Pelargonium × mariae contribute to several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from anxiety and stress. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which are used to support overall well-being and treat specific health conditions.
